item.title: Research
item.description:
Research Projects

My research addresses critical challenges in AI trustworthiness, privacy, and security — with a particular focus on real-world social and medical systems.

BotLens — Privacy Auditing for Third-Party Community Bots

BotLens is a privacy auditing framework that systematically analyzes how third-party community bots on social platforms collect, process, and expose user data. As bots become deeply integrated into online communities (Discord, Reddit, Slack), users often have little visibility into what data these bots access. BotLens surfaces these hidden privacy risks and informs better platform governance.

item.title: About
item.description:
About Me

I am a PhD student in Computer Science at The University of Alabama (Tuscaloosa), originally from Ghana. I am a member of the UA-SPECTRAL Lab, where my research focuses on trustworthy AI, data security, and the complex intersections between machine learning and human behavior.

Background

  • Origin: Ghana
  • Current Location: Tuscaloosa, Alabama, USA
  • Lab: UA-SPECTRAL Lab, The University of Alabama
  • Field: Computer Science (PhD)

Education

  • PhD in Computer Science, The University of Alabama, USA (In Progress)

Research Interests

  • Trustworthy AI and data security
  • Human-centered security and usable privacy
  • Adversarial machine learning
  • Natural Language Processing (NLP)
  • Third-party bot ecosystems on social platforms

Back to Home | Projects

Beyond Attack Success Rate: A Multi-Metric Evaluation of Adversarial Transferability in Medical Imaging Models E. Curl, K. Ampomah, M. Erfan, S. Dibbo Preprints, 2026

Topics: Adversarial ML · Medical Imaging · Trustworthy AI
